questions.q: In which year was the Third Battle of Panipat fought?
questions.explanation: The Third Battle of Panipat was fought in 1761 CE.

questions.q: The Third Battle of Panipat was fought between the Marathas and which invading force?
options: The Afghans under Ahmad Shah Durrani
questions.explanation: The Marathas fought the Afghan forces of Ahmad Shah Durrani (Abdali) at Panipat in 1761.

questions.q: What was the outcome of the Third Battle of Panipat for the Marathas?
options: A negotiated truce with no fighting
questions.explanation: The Marathas suffered a crushing defeat with very heavy losses at the Third Battle of Panipat.

questions.q: The Afghan leader at the Third Battle of Panipat, Ahmad Shah Durrani, is also commonly known as what?
questions.explanation: Ahmad Shah Durrani is also widely known as Ahmad Shah Abdali.

questions.q: The town of Panipat, site of the battle, lies in which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: Panipat is located in present-day Haryana, north of Delhi.

questions.q: The Third Battle of Panipat was one of the largest battles of which century?
questions.explanation: Fought in 1761, Panipat was one of the largest land battles of the 18th century.

questions.q: The Maratha defeat at Panipat temporarily checked their expansion in which direction?
questions.explanation: The defeat at Panipat checked Maratha expansion in northern India for a time.

questions.q: The Third Battle of Panipat was fought near which Mughal capital city?
questions.explanation: Panipat lies a short distance north of Delhi, the Mughal capital.

questions.q: The Marathas who fought at Panipat were part of which empire?
questions.explanation: The forces defeated at Panipat belonged to the Maratha Empire.

questions.q: Panipat had earlier been the site of two other major battles before 1761, the first being in which year?
questions.explanation: The First Battle of Panipat in 1526 saw Babur defeat Ibrahim Lodi, founding the Mughal Empire.

questions.q: Who was the overall Maratha commander at the Third Battle of Panipat?
questions.explanation: The Maratha army at Panipat was commanded by Sadashiv Rao Bhau, cousin of the Peshwa.

questions.q: Which young Maratha prince, son of the Peshwa, commanded a wing and died at Panipat?
questions.explanation: Vishwasrao, the young son of Peshwa Balaji Baji Rao, was killed at Panipat, a blow that shook the Maratha line.

questions.q: Who was the reigning Maratha Peshwa at the time of the Third Battle of Panipat?
questions.explanation: Balaji Baji Rao, known as Nana Saheb, was the Peshwa during the Panipat campaign of 1760-61.

questions.q: Which Maratha cavalry commander, of the Holkar house, withdrew from the field during the battle?
questions.explanation: Malhar Rao Holkar withdrew his cavalry from the field at Panipat, a controversial move during the rout.

questions.q: Ahmad Shah Durrani was allied at Panipat with which Indian Muslim power based in Awadh?
questions.explanation: Shuja-ud-Daulah, the Nawab of Awadh, allied with Ahmad Shah Durrani against the Marathas at Panipat.

questions.q: Ahmad Shah Durrani was also joined at Panipat by which Rohilla Afghan leader of the Doab region?
questions.explanation: Najib-ud-Daulah, leader of the Rohilla Afghans, was a key ally of Durrani at Panipat.

questions.q: In which month and year was the main Third Battle of Panipat fought?
questions.explanation: The decisive Third Battle of Panipat was fought on 14 January 1761.

questions.q: Before the main battle, the Marathas had earlier captured which Mughal capital city in 1760?
questions.explanation: The Marathas under Sadashiv Rao Bhau captured Delhi in 1760 before the campaign culminated at Panipat.

questions.q: One important reason for the Maratha defeat at Panipat was a severe shortage of what in their encamped army?
questions.explanation: The Maratha army, encircled and cut off, suffered a crippling shortage of food and supplies before the battle.

questions.q: The Maratha artillery and trained infantry at Panipat were partly organised by which commander known for European-style troops?
questions.explanation: Ibrahim Khan Gardi commanded the Maratha artillery and French-trained infantry units at Panipat.

questions.q: Following the battle, which Maratha leader notably survived but was wounded, later restoring power in the north?
questions.explanation: Mahadji Scindia survived Panipat with a wound and later rebuilt Maratha power in northern India.

questions.q: The Maratha Peshwa Balaji Baji Rao died shortly after Panipat, reportedly broken by the news; he died in which year?
questions.explanation: Balaji Baji Rao died in June 1761, only months after learning of the catastrophe at Panipat.

questions.q: The Maratha army at Panipat was hampered by the presence of a very large number of what alongside the soldiers?
questions.explanation: A huge body of non-combatant pilgrims and camp followers travelled with the Maratha army, straining supplies and mobility.

questions.q: Which Jat ruler initially supported the Maratha campaign but withdrew his help before the final battle?
questions.explanation: Suraj Mal, the Jat ruler of Bharatpur, parted ways with Sadashiv Rao Bhau over strategy before the battle.

questions.q: The decisive battle at Panipat fell on the festival of which occasion in January 1761?
questions.explanation: The Third Battle of Panipat was fought on 14 January 1761, the day of Makar Sankranti.

questions.q: After his victory, the Afghan ruler Ahmad Shah Durrani chose to do what rather than seize permanent control of Delhi?
questions.explanation: Despite winning, Ahmad Shah Durrani soon withdrew to Afghanistan rather than ruling India directly, owing to unrest among his own troops.

questions.q: The fate of the Maratha commander Sadashiv Rao Bhau after the battle was that he was what?
questions.explanation: Sadashiv Rao Bhau was killed in the fighting at Panipat; his body was identified afterward among the slain.

questions.q: Within roughly a decade, the Maratha recovery in the north after Panipat was led under which young Peshwa?
questions.explanation: Peshwa Madhavrao I led the Maratha resurgence in the years after Panipat, regaining much lost ground in the north.

questions.q: The conflict at Panipat in 1761 grew partly out of Maratha intervention in the affairs of which province seized from the Afghans in 1758?
questions.explanation: Maratha expansion into the Punjab, taking Lahore from Afghan control in 1758, helped provoke Durrani's invasion.

questions.q: Ibrahim Khan Gardi, the Maratha artillery commander, met what fate after the battle?
options: Captured and put to death by the Afghans
questions.explanation: Ibrahim Khan Gardi was captured after Panipat and executed by the victorious Afghan side.
